title: excoecaria eglandulosa
intro: Excoecaria eglandulosa, also known as the Milky Mangrove or Poison Tree, is a plant species native to tropical regions of the Pacific and Indian Oceans. The plant contains toxic compounds, particularly phorbol esters, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Excoecaria eglandulosa include its potential to treat various conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as a local anesthetic. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although caution is advised due to its toxic compounds and potential side effects.
